data.title: RLI Corporation: Strong Q4 Boosted by Low Cat Losses, but Slowing Premium Growth and Rising Loss Costs Justify Hold Rating
data.description:
William Blair analyst Adam Klauber has maintained their neutral stance on RLI stock, giving a Hold rating today.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Adam Klauber has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to RLI’s current performance and near-term outlook. While the company delivered fourth-quarter operating EPS meaningfully above consensus, this upside was largely attributable to unusually low catastrophe losses rather than sustainable operating improvements. At the same time, premium growth has softened, with overall written premiums declining as weakness in the property segment more than offsets modest expansion in casualty lines. Klauber expects only low- to mid-single-digit top-line growth through 2026, even assuming a later recovery in both casualty and property, which tempers the growth profile.

Klauber also notes that underwriting profitability showed some deterioration, as both the expense ratio and underlying loss ratio moved higher despite RLI’s conservative stance. He anticipates continued upward pressure on loss costs, driven by rising claim severity, a more challenging legal environment in casualty, an unfavorable shift in business mix toward higher-loss casualty lines, and less benefit from reserve releases. Although he believes RLI can maintain a combined ratio in the high 80s, this would still translate into earnings contraction in 2026. Given these headwinds to forward earnings growth and the balance of risk versus reward, he maintains a Market Perform (Hold) rating on the shares.

In another report released today, Jefferies also upgraded the stock to a Hold with a $52.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Intuitive Surgical: Durable Growth, Operating Leverage, and Premium-Valuation Upside Through 2026
data.description:
William Blair analyst Brandon Vazquez has maintained their bullish stance on ISRG stock, giving a Buy rating on January 20.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Brandon Vazquez has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight Intuitive Surgical’s durable growth profile and earnings momentum. He notes that the company delivered a solid fourth quarter, with revenue matching already strong preliminary figures and earnings per share substantially exceeding market expectations, driven by ongoing strength in core procedure volumes and growing adoption of newer platforms like da Vinci 5, Ion, and SP. Vazquez emphasizes that the company is expanding its addressable market through recent procedural clearances in cardiac and breast surgery, advancing new imaging capabilities, and building inventory of newer systems positioned to penetrate ambulatory surgery centers and price-sensitive international regions.

At the same time, he underscores that as these platforms scale and new products are commercialized, Intuitive is demonstrating significant operating leverage, supporting roughly 20% annual EPS growth and underpinning his view of sustained upside into 2026 and beyond. He characterizes the story as one of the most resilient growth cases in the medtech space, supported by multiple structural tailwinds and upcoming catalysts. While acknowledging that investor sentiment and headlines around emerging competitors represent the primary stock risk, Vazquez believes the company’s strong fundamentals and execution will continue to outweigh these concerns, justifying a Buy rating even at a premium valuation multiple to 2026 earnings.

In another report released on January 20, Piper Sandler also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $610.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
